## Summary

Adds `novelette` (7,500–19,999 words) and `novella` (20,000–49,999 words) tiers to the `InputScale` taxonomy, aligning with standard publishing classifications. Adds a "Manuscript Structure" radio toggle to the evaluation submission form so authors can declare whether their submission is a standalone story or chapter(s) from a larger work. This distinction determines whether the system classifies submissions in the 7,500–49,999 word range as complete works (novelette/novella) or multi-chapter excerpts.

## Scope

**Changed files:**
- `lib/evaluation/signal/scopePolicy.ts` — Added `novelette` and `novella` to `InputScale` union type; added scope policies for both tiers; bumped policy version to v2
- `lib/evaluation/pipeline/submissionScope.ts` — Added `ManuscriptStructure` type; updated word count boundaries; `classifySubmissionScope` accepts optional `manuscriptStructure` parameter
- `lib/evaluation/signal/manuscriptClaimPolicy.ts` — `novella` now routes as `LONG_FORM` (manuscript-wide claim scope)
- `lib/config/evaluationRuntimeConfig.ts` — Added `novelette`/`novella` to `TimeoutScopeInputScale`; both get long-form timeout floors
- `schemas/evaluation-result-v2.ts` — Added new scale values to `input_scale` union
- `components/evaluation/ManuscriptSubmissionForm.jsx` — Added "Manuscript Structure" radio buttons with hover tooltip; `manuscript_structure` included in job payload; `getEvaluationMode` updated for new tiers

**Out of scope:**
- base44 functions (legacy, separate scale system)
- API handler changes for persisting `manuscript_structure` (follow-up)
- 9th Story Ledger layer for Identity & Pronoun Verification (separate PR)

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- `manuscriptStructure` defaults to `"chapters"` for backward compatibility — existing submissions without the field behave identically to before
- `novelette` tier gets HIGH confidence on `narrativeClosure` (since it's a complete work), which differs from `multi_chapter` where `narrativeClosure` is MODERATE
- The `manuscript_structure` field is sent in the job payload but the API handler does not yet persist it — follow-up needed to thread it through to `classifySubmissionScope`

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: N/A — this changes pipeline classification logic (InputScale taxonomy) and adds new scope tiers that affect confidence caps, evaluation routing, and timeout scaling
